summary: Matched two Zebes stage functions in src/melee/gr/grzebes.c: grZebes_801D84A4, a stage initialization routine, and grZebes_801DCCC8, a small float-return helper using Ground_801C4368 and the grZe_804DB0B0 global. The PR also added the grZebes_801D84A4 prototype to grzebes.h and declared needed static/global symbols in grzebes.c. The author stated this was split from PR #2024 as a focused Zebes stage matching PR.
what_changed: Implemented grZebes_801D84A4, replacing a placeholder marker with a concrete stage initialization sequence.
grZebes_801D84A4 stores the result of Ground_801C49F8() in static HSD_GObj* grZe_804D6990.
grZebes_801D84A4 sets stage_info.unk8C.b4 to 0 and stage_info.unk8C.b5 to 1.
grZebes_801D84A4 calls grZebes_801D8558 with stage object IDs 0, 1, 6, 4, and 8, then calls Ground_801C39C0(), Ground_801C3BB4(), and grZebes_801DA3E8().
Implemented grZebes_801DCCC8 as a float-returning helper that calls Ground_801C4368(&slope, &intercept) and returns grZe_804DB0B0 + slope.
Added a static forward declaration for grZebes_801D8558(int).
Added a static file-scope declaration for HSD_GObj* grZe_804D6990 and an extern f32 declaration for grZe_804DB0B0.
Added the public prototype for grZebes_801D84A4(void) to src/melee/gr/grzebes.h.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to two Zebes matches after splitting from a larger PR, making the change easy to review and verify.
Used an explicit static prototype for grZebes_801D8558(int) before grZebes_801D84A4 needed it, supporting builds with --require-protos.
Matched grZebes_801DCCC8 with local float temporaries passed by address to Ground_801C4368, preserving the apparent ABI/dataflow even though only slope is used in the return.
Added the grZebes_801D84A4 header declaration alongside the existing grZebes_801DCCC8 declaration, reflecting that the stage init function is referenced outside this C file.
decomp_lessons: For stage init matches, reproduce the exact ordered sequence of Ground helper calls, stage_info bitfield assignments, and per-object setup calls; call order is likely material for matching.

Small float helper functions may require keeping seemingly-unused out-parameters, such as intercept from Ground_801C4368, because the callee interface writes multiple values even if the matched function returns only one.
When matching with --require-protos, add both static forward declarations and exported header prototypes as soon as a newly implemented function depends on them.
Stage globals may need local declarations with the correct storage class: this PR used static HSD_GObj* for grZe_804D6990 in grzebes.c and extern f32 for grZe_804DB0B0.
naming_conventions: Zebes stage functions use the grZebes_801DXXXX address-based naming convention.
Zebes file-scope/global data symbols use the grZe_804DXXXX or grZe_804DBXXX address-based naming convention.
Ground engine helpers remain named Ground_801CXXXX when still address-based.
Stage object setup helper grZebes_801D8558 takes an int object/index argument, inferred from its new prototype and call sites.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted placeholder comments for grZebes_801D84A4 and grZebes_801DCCC8 into C implementations that the PR body reports as 100% matched.
Matched a stage initialization function by assigning a global object pointer, setting bitfields, and emitting a fixed sequence of helper calls with immediate integer arguments.
Matched a simple float-return function by allocating two local f32 variables, passing both addresses to Ground_801C4368, and returning a global float offset plus the computed slope.
No assembly diff, inline asm, register-allocation note, or reviewer matching discussion was present in the provided slice.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body test plan says the build passed with --require-protos; CI was listed as not yet checked in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find PR #2024 to understand the larger Zebes matching context this PR was split from.
Search for later renames or typing improvements for stage_info.unk8C.b4 and stage_info.unk8C.b5.
Search for later documentation of grZe_804DB0B0 and whether it is specifically an acid/global vertical offset.
Look for subsequent matches around grZebes_801D8558 and grZebes_801DA3E8 to identify the object IDs passed by grZebes_801D84A4.
